# How LLMs Work — Interactive Walkthrough

> An interactive walkthrough of Karpathy's microgpt: a complete GPT implementation in 200 lines of pure Python, with no dependencies.
> https://howllmswork-4fw.pages.dev

## What this is

This site teaches how large language models work from the ground up, by walking through every line of microgpt.py — a 200-line Python script that trains a character-level GPT on 32,033 baby names and generates new ones. The same algorithm powers ChatGPT, just at a different scale.

## Source material

- Blog post: https://karpathy.github.io/2026/02/12/microgpt/
- Code (gist): https://gist.github.com/karpathy/8627fe009c40f57531cb18360106ce95
- Google Colab: https://colab.research.google.com/drive/1vyN5zo6rqUp_dYNbT4Yrco66zuWCZKoN?usp=sharing

## Walkthrough Steps

### Context
1. **The Task** — What microgpt does and why it matters
2. **The Data** — 32,033 baby names — each one a "document"

### Preprocessing
3. **Tokenize** — Text → integers

### Learning Engine
4. **Backprop** — Compute gradients

### Architecture
5. **Parameters** — The model's knowledge
6. **Embed** — IDs → vectors
7. **Attend** — Tokens communicate
8. **Transform** — MLP computation

### Training
9. **Train** — Predict → loss → backprop → update
10. **Update** — Adjust parameters (Adam)

### Using the Model
11. **Predict** — Generate new text
12. **The Complete Picture** — Everything else is just efficiency

## Glossary (41 terms)

- **Text to Numbers** (5 terms): Token, Vocabulary, BOS (Beginning of Sequence), BPE (Byte Pair Encoding), Embedding
- **Model Structure** (9 terms): Parameter, Weight / Weight Matrix, State Dict, Vector, Dimension (n_embd), MLP (Multilayer Perceptron), ReLU (Rectified Linear Unit), Residual Connection (Skip Connection), RMSNorm (Root Mean Square Normalization)
- **Attention Mechanism** (5 terms): Attention, Query, Key, Value (Q, K, V), Attention Head, KV Cache, Softmax
- **Learning & Training** (11 terms): Gradient, Backpropagation (Backward Pass), Chain Rule, Autograd (Automatic Differentiation), Value (Autograd Wrapper), Loss (Cross-Entropy Loss), Forward Pass, Training Loop, Adam Optimizer, Learning Rate, Epoch / Training Step
- **Generation & Output** (5 terms): Logit, Inference (Generation), Temperature, Sampling, Hallucination
- **The Big Picture** (6 terms): Layer (Transformer Layer), Context Window (Block Size), Transformer, GPT (Generative Pre-trained Transformer), Post-Training (SFT + RLHF), Tensor

## Scale Comparison: microgpt vs Production LLMs

| Dimension | microgpt | Production |
|-----------|----------|------------|
| Parameters | 4,192 | 100B – 1T+ |
| Layers | 1 | 80 – 120+ |
| Embedding dim | 16 | 8,192 – 12,288 |
| Context window | 16 tokens | 128K – 1M+ |
| Vocabulary | 27 chars | 100K – 200K tokens |
| Training data | 32K names | Trillions of tokens |
| Compute | Python scalars | Thousands of GPUs |
| Tokenizer | Character-level | BPE / SentencePiece |
| Post-training | None | SFT + RLHF |

## Full content

For the complete narratives, code annotations, and glossary definitions, see:
https://howllmswork-4fw.pages.dev/llms-full.txt
